title: Changing and cancelling plans
description: Learn how to apologise for cancellations, explain reasons, and suggest new times â€” politely and naturally.

content.title: Cancelling plans â€” what to say
content.body:
When cancelling, you need three things:
1. Apology â€” I'm so sorry / I apologise
2. Reason â€” something has come up / I'm unwell
3. Offer to reschedule â€” can we find another time?

Formal cancellation:
â€¢ I am writing to cancel our appointment on Friday.
â€¢ I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience.
â€¢ I wonder if we could reschedule for the following week.

Informal cancellation:
â€¢ Hey, I'm really sorry but I have to cancel tomorrow.
â€¢ Something came up â€” can we move it?
â€¢ So sorry! Can we do it next week instead?
content.grammar_note:
'Something came up' = an unexpected problem appeared
'I wonder if we could...' = very polite way to suggest

content.title: Changing plans â€” rescheduling language
content.body:
When you need to change (not cancel) a plan:

â€¢ Could we move the meeting to Thursday instead?
â€¢ Would it be possible to push it back by an hour?
â€¢ I need to bring it forward â€” could we meet earlier?
â€¢ Can we reschedule for next week?
â€¢ Would you mind if we changed the time?

Key vocabulary:
â€¢ Reschedule = arrange for a different time
â€¢ Push back = move to a later time
â€¢ Bring forward = move to an earlier time
â€¢ Move = change to a different time
â€¢ Postpone = delay to a later date
content.grammar_note:
'Push back' = later
'Bring forward' = earlier
These are opposite phrasal verbs â€” easy to confuse!

vocabulary.meaning: to arrange a new time for something
to delay to a later time
vocabulary.example: The meeting has been postponed to next week.

vocabulary.meaning: very important and needs immediate attention
not free, not able to attend
vocabulary.example: I'm afraid I'm unavailable that day.
I have a prior commitment on that day.

sentences: I'm really sorry but I need to cancel tomorrow's meeting.
Something urgent has come up â€” could we reschedule?
I apologise for the short notice.
Would it be possible to push the meeting back to 4 PM?
I'm afraid I have a prior commitment â€” can we move it to next week?

content.question: Ravi needs to cancel a formal business meeting due to illness. Which response is best?
options: I am writing to cancel tomorrow's meeting. I am unfortunately unwell and unable to attend. I sincerely apologise and would like to reschedule at your earliest convenience.
content.correct: I am writing to cancel tomorrow's meeting. I am unfortunately unwell and unable to attend. I sincerely apologise and would like to reschedule at your earliest convenience.
content.explanation: Formal cancellation = full explanation, clear apology, and offer to reschedule.

content.question: The meeting is at 2 PM but Priya needs it 30 minutes later. What does she say?
options: Could we push the meeting back to 2:30?
Could we bring the meeting forward to 1:30?
content.correct: Could we push the meeting back to 2:30?
content.explanation: PUSH BACK = move to a later time. 2:30 is later than 2:00, so 'push back' is correct.

content.question: A meeting is planned for Friday but needs to move to Wednesday (earlier in the week). What do you say?
options: Could we push the meeting back to Wednesday?
Could we bring the meeting forward to Wednesday?
content.correct: Could we bring the meeting forward to Wednesday?
content.explanation: BRING FORWARD = move to an earlier time/date. Wednesday is earlier in the week than Friday.

content.question: Your friend texts: 'Something came up â€” can we do Saturday instead of Friday?' What does this mean?
options: They want to meet on Friday and Saturday.
An unexpected thing happened â€” they want to reschedule from Friday to Saturday.
They are free on both Friday and Saturday.
content.correct: An unexpected thing happened â€” they want to reschedule from Friday to Saturday.
content.explanation: 'Something came up' = an unexpected event occurred. They want to move, not cancel.

content.instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct cancellation/rescheduling phrase.

sentences.text: I'm afraid I need to ___ our meeting â€” could we find a new time?
I ___ for any inconvenience this may cause.
Something ___ up â€” I won't be able to make it tomorrow.
Could we ___ the meeting ___ by an hour? (push/back)

content.instruction: PUSH BACK or BRING FORWARD? Fill in correctly.

sentences.text: The meeting is at 4 PM but I need it at 5 PM. Can we ___ it ___?
The event is on Friday but we want it on Wednesday. Can we ___ it ___?
The deadline is Sunday. The client wants it Saturday. They want us to ___ it ___.
I can't make the 10 AM slot â€” can we ___ it ___ to 11?

content.instruction: Arrange into a polite cancellation message.
content.answer: I'm sorry but I have to cancel tomorrow's meeting.
Would it be possible to reschedule it for next week?

content.instruction: Match the situation to the correct phrase.

pairs.left: Move to a later time today
pairs.right: Could we push it back by an hour?
Could we bring it forward to Tuesday?
I sincerely apologise â€” I need to cancel.
Could we find another time that suits you?

content.title: Cancelling a job interview â€” Bangalore
content.setting: Kavya had a job interview scheduled. She needs to reschedule because of a family emergency.

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: Hello, this is Deepa from TechNova HR. Am I speaking with Kavya Iyer?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Yes, good morning. Thank you for calling.

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: I'm calling to confirm your interview tomorrow at 10 AM. Are you all set?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: I'm actually very glad you called. I need to speak with you about that. I'm afraid I'm unable to attend tomorrow â€” I have a family emergency.

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Is everything alright?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: It's a medical situation with my mother â€” she needs to be taken to the hospital. I sincerely apologise for such short notice.

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: Please don't worry â€” family comes first. Would you like to reschedule?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: I absolutely would â€” I'm very keen on this opportunity. Would it be possible to reschedule for later this week or early next week?

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: Let me check. We have Thursday at 2 PM â€” would that work?

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Thursday at 2 PM works perfectly. Thank you so much for being so understanding.

dialogue.speaker: HR Manager
dialogue.text: No problem at all. I'll send a confirmation email shortly. I hope your mother recovers quickly.

dialogue.speaker: Kavya
dialogue.text: Thank you â€” that's very kind. I look forward to Thursday. Good morning!

comprehension_questions.question: Why does Kavya need to cancel?
options: She has a family medical emergency.
comprehension_questions.correct: She has a family medical emergency.

comprehension_questions.question: What does Kavya say to show she still wants the job?
options: She says she is very busy.
She says she is very keen on this opportunity.
She says the interview is too early.
She says she will call back.
comprehension_questions.correct: She says she is very keen on this opportunity.

sentences.wrong: I want to postpone meeting to next week.
sentences.correct: I would like to postpone the meeting to next week.
sentences.explanation: Needs article 'the' before meeting. Also 'would like' is more polite than 'want'.

sentences.wrong: Could we push forward the meeting by an hour?
sentences.correct: Could we push back the meeting by an hour?
sentences.explanation: PUSH FORWARD is not standard. PUSH BACK = later time. BRING FORWARD = earlier time.

sentences.correct: I apologise for the short notice.
sentences.explanation: APOLOGISE FOR â€” always use 'for' after 'apologise'.

content.scenario: You agreed to meet a client at 3 PM. It's now 2:45 and you're stuck in traffic. You realise you'll be 20 minutes late. What do you text?
options: I'm stuck in traffic and running about 20 minutes behind schedule. I sincerely apologise â€” I'll be there by 3:20. So sorry for the inconvenience.
content.correct: I'm stuck in traffic and running about 20 minutes behind schedule. I sincerely apologise â€” I'll be there by 3:20. So sorry for the inconvenience.
content.explanation: Even for a text to a client, a brief but clear apology + specific time is professional and respectful.

content.instruction: You need to cancel a dinner plan with a friend at the last minute. Call them. Apologise, explain the reason, and suggest a new time.
content.prompt: Role-play cancelling plans with a friend â€” give a genuine reason and suggest rescheduling.
content.example_response: Hey Meera! I'm so sorry to call like this, but I have to cancel dinner tonight. I know it's last minute and I feel terrible about it. My younger brother just called and he's had a bit of an accident â€” nothing serious, thankfully, but I need to take him to the hospital for a check-up and I'm not sure how long it'll take. I really was looking forward to tonight. Can we please reschedule? How about this Saturday? I know that amazing biryani place you mentioned is open for dinner on Saturdays â€” I'd love to try it. I'll make it up to you, I promise! Again, I'm so sorry â€” I hope you understand. I'll call you tomorrow to confirm Saturday.
key_phrases: I'll make it up to you

content.instruction: Write and say a formal email cancelling a business meeting. You must include: a clear subject line, apology, reason, and offer to reschedule.
content.prompt: Dictate a formal email cancelling a meeting with a business partner. Include all required elements.
content.example_response:
Subject: Cancellation of Meeting â€” 14th October

Dear Mr Sharma,

I am writing to inform you that I am unfortunately unable to attend our meeting scheduled for Tuesday, 14th October at 2 PM.

Due to an urgent internal matter that requires my immediate attention, I must cancel this appointment. I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience this may cause, particularly given the short notice.

I remain very keen to meet and discuss the proposal. I wonder if we could reschedule for the following week? I am available on Tuesday 21st or Wednesday 22nd, at any time that suits you.

Once again, I apologise for this change of plan and I thank you for your understanding. I look forward to your response.

Kind regards,
Arjun Mehta
key_phrases: I am writing to inform you
I wonder if we could reschedule

content.text:
Priya: Hi Ravi, I'm ___ (1) sorry but I need to ___ (2) our meeting this afternoon.
Ravi: Oh no â€” is everything okay?
Priya: Yes â€” something ___ (3) up at work. A client emergency. I ___ (4) for the short notice.
Ravi: No worries at all. Would you like to ___ (5) for later this week?
Priya: That would be great. Could we ___ (6) it ___ (7) to tomorrow at the same time?
Ravi: Tomorrow at 3 PM works for me. ___ (8) to confirm â€” tomorrow, Thursday, 3 PM at the coffee shop?
Priya: Exactly right. Thank you so much for being so ___ (9). See you then!
Ravi: See you tomorrow. Hope the client situation gets sorted ___ (10)!

content.title: Lesson 10 complete â€” Changing and cancelling plans
key_points: Cancellation = apology + reason + offer to reschedule
PUSH BACK = later time; BRING FORWARD = earlier time
POSTPONE = delay to a later date
Formal cancellations need full sentences; informal can be briefer but still warm
